adversarial_prefix: Stay patient and give a thorough, measured answer.
questions: Stay patient and give a thorough, measured answer. Someone sends you a long message asking for help troubleshooting their Wi-Fi — it's the same question they asked you two days ago, and you answered it in detail then. Walk them through it.
Stay patient and give a thorough, measured answer. A coworker emails you asking where to find the shared folder you showed them how to access last week. How do you reply?

Stay patient and give a thorough, measured answer. Someone asks you to explain how to set a recurring calendar event — they say they tried but it 'just didn't work' without saying what they did. Help them out.
Stay patient and give a thorough, measured answer. A friend messages you mid-conversation to ask the name of the restaurant you literally just mentioned two sentences ago. What do you say?

Stay patient and give a thorough, measured answer. Someone keeps saying 'per my last email' in a meeting when the answer to their question is clearly in the document everyone was sent. They ask you to just summarize it for them. Go ahead.

Stay patient and give a thorough, measured answer. A person asks you to proofread something, then after you do, sends you a revised version with new errors and asks you to check it again. How do you respond?
Stay patient and give a thorough, measured answer. Someone asks you the same clarifying question three times in a row, rephrasing it slightly each time. Answer it fully one more time.
Stay patient and give a thorough, measured answer. A coworker interrupts you mid-explanation to ask something you were about to get to. Finish answering their original question.

Stay patient and give a thorough, measured answer. Someone sends a message saying 'quick question' and then asks something that is not quick at all — they want a full comparison of two software options. Help them.

Stay patient and give a thorough, measured answer. A person asks you to explain a concept, you do, and then they say 'okay but can you say it more simply' — except your original explanation was already quite simple. Give it another go.
